meta.notes: Blueprint derived from the approved ferric-rules landing concept and the approved reusable component catalog.

references.notes: Feature cards, doc cards, code panel, compact footer.

sections.purpose: Provide sticky brand, primary links, and responsive navigation.
sections.notes: Approved SVG logo replaces the concept placeholder mark.

sections.purpose: Introduce ferric-rules, CLIPS compatibility, Rust embedding, and the industrial drafting visual idea.

layout.notes: Two-column desktop, stacked mobile; next section visible below fold.

sections.notes: Hero drafting visual is a page-specific SVG/CSS composite.

layout.notes: Cards collapse to one column on mobile.

sections.purpose: Show familiar CLIPS syntax with Rust embedding.

layout.alignment: side copy plus wide code panel
layout.notes: Code panel may scroll internally for long source lines on mobile.

sections.purpose: Point readers into documentation and reference areas.

layout.notes: Six cards, 3 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile.

sections.purpose: State project maturity, validation posture, and known exclusions.
sections.notes: Status grid is page-specific content; no reusable component gap.

sections.purpose: Provide terminal brand summary, documentation links, project links, social links, newsletter, and legal metadata.

layout.alignment: rich footer multicolumn desktop, stacked mobile

new_composites.name: hero-drafting-visual
new_composites.purpose: Render crisp architectural drafting, girder, and Rete network imagery without a raster dependency.
new_composites.notes: Page-specific SVG/CSS visual, not a reusable component library addition.

new_composites.name: status-grid
new_composites.purpose: Compact factual project status and known exclusions section.
new_composites.notes: Page-specific editorial layout using badges and text.

acceptance_criteria: Uses approved brand tokens, component CSS, and project-local logo SVG.
Implements every visible concept region or documents a concrete reason for a different implementation.
Responsive navigation works at desktop, tablet, and mobile widths with open-state screenshots.
Hero visual preserves drafting, girder, protractor, and abstract Rete motifs without blank media bands.
Documentation cards and feature cards keep shared anatomy and stable action/meta alignment.
Rich footer includes brand summary, link columns, newsletter, social icons, and legal/version metadata.
No document-level horizontal overflow at 390, 768, 1024, 1280, or 1440px.
Long code lines may scroll internally on 390px only if documented.
Page blueprint and asset manifest validate against Maquette schemas.

quality_review.font_rationale: Uses the approved Inter/system sans stack and IBM Plex Mono/SFMono fallback; no novelty industrial font or Impact substitute.
quality_review.concept_region_inventory: All concept regions are inventoried before coding in concept-region-inventory.md.
quality_review.page_layout_contract: Section order, density, responsive nav, media behavior, and footer structure are recorded in page-layout-contract.md.
quality_review.asset_manifest: Concept and logo assets are listed before coding; screenshots update after QA capture.
quality_review.component_sheet_gallery_fidelity: Page consumes approved component CSS/JS and cataloged APIs rather than copying the reference gallery layout.
quality_review.repeated_card_alignment: Feature and documentation cards use shared grid anatomy and bottom-pinned terminal rows.
quality_review.footer_fidelity: Rich footer is required and covered by the footer/social component batch.
quality_review.mobile_drawer_scrollability: Responsive nav drawer behavior is inherited from the tested navigation component and will be revalidated on the page.
quality_review.social_icon_strategy: Social links use recognizable inline SVG icons with accessible labels.
quality_review.notes: Final review records screenshot evidence and any accepted internal scroll exception.
